---
title: "Evidence-Based Engineering"
toc: false
---

## Environmental Engineering • Applied Statistics • Reproducible Technical Reporting

Welcome to **Evidence-Based Engineering**, a public knowledge base dedicated to advancing environmental engineering through evidence-based methods, applied statistics, and reproducible technical reporting.

This website shares generalized engineering frameworks, educational resources, and analytical workflows for engineers, students, researchers, and technical professionals.

::: {.callout-note}
## Educational and Professional Resource

This website is intended for educational and professional development purposes. It presents generalized engineering methods, statistical techniques, and reproducible workflows. Any examples are illustrative and do not represent specific facilities, organizations, clients, suppliers, manufacturers, technology proponents, or regulatory assessments.
:::

---

## Featured Knowledge Areas

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### Treatment Performance Assessment

Frameworks for assessing wastewater treatment system performance using engineering principles, long-term monitoring data, applied statistics, and reproducible reporting.

[View framework](projects/treatment-performance-assessment/)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Engineering Statistics

Practical statistical methods for environmental and civil engineering, including uncertainty, reliability, visualization, and interpretation.

[Explore methods](projects/)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Reproducible Reporting

Use of Quarto, R, Python, Git, and GitHub to create transparent, repeatable, and well-documented technical analyses.

[View projects](projects/)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Teaching and Mentoring

Resources for students, engineers, and early-career professionals interested in applying engineering judgment and statistical thinking.

[Learn more](about.qmd)
:::

:::

---

## Featured Projects

- **Treatment Performance Assessment Framework**
  - Generalized methods for evaluating wastewater treatment system performance.

- **Engineering Statistics**
  - Practical statistical methods for environmental engineering.

- **Reproducible Reporting**
  - Quarto, Python, R, and Git workflows for transparent technical reporting.

- **Technology Assessment Framework**
  - Evidence-based approaches for evaluating environmental technologies.


## Guiding Principle

> Engineering decisions are strongest when they are supported by transparent evidence, reproducible analysis, and clear communication of uncertainty.

---

## Website Roadmap

This site will gradually include:

- treatment performance assessment frameworks;
- engineering statistics tutorials;
- reproducible Quarto, R, and Python workflows;
- public research and publications;
- teaching and mentoring resources;
- reviews of methods, tools, and technical resources.

---

## Current Status

The website is under active development.

Future additions include:

- Publications
- Teaching resources
- Technical tutorials
- Engineering case studies using synthetic or public data
- Downloadable reference material
